turns.content: Hi! I'm here to help you set up your new project. Let's start with the basics — what are you building?

I'm building a tic-tac-toe game. It's a web app where you can play against an AI opponent. The twist is the AI adapts to your skill level — it starts easy and gets harder as you get better. I also want a way to share games with friends through a link, kind of like how you share a Google Doc.
That sounds fun! An adaptive AI opponent and link-based sharing are interesting features. Who's building this — just you, or is there a team or organization behind it?
Just me. It's a personal project. I'm doing it to learn and to have something fun to show in my portfolio.
Great — a portfolio project with real substance. What tech stack are you thinking? Any languages or frameworks you've already decided on?

React for the frontend. Node.js for the backend. I want to use WebSockets for real-time game state sync when sharing with friends. For the tunnel-based sharing I'm thinking cloudflared or something similar — I haven't decided exactly which tunneling tool yet.

Solid choices. React + Node.js with WebSockets for real-time sync. And you're considering cloudflared for the sharing tunnel but keeping that decision open. What about the AI opponent — any thoughts on how that would work technically?

I want to use minimax with alpha-beta pruning. The adaptive part would work by limiting the search depth based on a difficulty level, and the difficulty adjusts based on the player's win/loss ratio. So if you're winning a lot, the AI searches deeper and plays better.
Nice — minimax with dynamic depth as the difficulty lever. That's elegant. Are there any constraints or requirements you have in mind? Performance targets, browser support, that kind of thing?

It should work on all modern browsers — Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge. No IE support needed. Performance-wise, the AI should respond in under 200ms even at max difficulty. Since it's tic-tac-toe the game tree is small enough that shouldn't be a problem. I also want it to work without requiring any login or account creation.
Makes sense — modern browsers, fast AI response, no auth required. What would success look like for this project? When would you consider it "done"?

Done means I can play a game against the AI, share it with a friend via link so they can watch in real time, and the AI noticeably adjusts to my skill level over multiple games. It should be deployed somewhere publicly accessible so I can put it in my portfolio.
Clear success criteria. Is there anything you specifically want to keep out of scope? Things you've thought about but decided not to include?

No multiplayer PvP — it's only against the AI. No mobile app, web only. No user accounts or persistent profiles. No board sizes other than 3x3. No tournaments or matchmaking. I want to keep it focused.
Good boundaries. Any preferences for how the code should be organized? Testing approach, coding style, anything like that?

I prefer functional programming style where possible. Pure functions for game logic, immutable state. TypeScript for type safety on both frontend and backend. I want good test coverage for the AI logic since that's the interesting part.
